channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Judges punishing Jan. 6 rioters say they fear more political violence as Election Day nears
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Over the past four years, judges at Washington’s federal courthouse have punished hundreds of rioters who stormed the U.S. Capitol in an unprecedented assault on the nation's democracy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: States to election officials: Follow the law or face consequences
item.description: In at least five battleground states, officials have investigated, indicted and even jailed officials who tried to interfere with the vote count.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: A failed mic leaves Donald Trump pacing the stage in silence for nearly 20 minutes
item.description: Donald Trump paced his rally stage in silence for nearly 20 minutes Friday night in Detroit after his microphone cut out.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden administration races to shell out billions for clean energy as election nears
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration is shelling out billions of dollars for clean energy and approving major offshore wind projects as officials race to secure major climate initiatives before President Joe Biden's term comes to an end.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT and MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pro-Trump yard sign leads to shooting in Las Vegas neighborhood
item.description: Two men were playing about the former president titled "[Expletive] Donald Trump" when they decided to park their car outside the pro-Trump house.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: What's a 'Jezebel spirit'? Some Christians use the term to paint Kamala Harris with a demonic brush
item.description: Christian nationalist leaders are telling followers that Vice President Kamala Harris is under the influence of a “Jezebel spirit,” using a term with deeply racist and misogynistic roots that is setting off alarm bells for religious and political scholars.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT and MICHELLE R. SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia Republicans readying post-election legal strategy
item.description: Georgia GOP Chairman Josh McKoon spoke with Fox News Digital about his party's legal strategy both before and after Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump earned money from government sources through Washington hotel, Democrats find
item.description: Over 11 months while former President Trump was in office, his Washington, D.C., hotel took in some $300,000 from sources inside and outside of government, including tens of thousands in payments from the U.S. Secret Service.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Nevada Senate debate
item.description: Sen. Jacky Rosen (D-Nev.) and Republican Sam Brown duked it out during their first and only debate Thursday night, making their pitches to voters in one of the key battleground states on the 2024 map and a must-win state for Democratic Senate majority hopes.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Trump and Harris are campaigning for votes in pivotal Michigan
item.description: With just a matter of a few weeks left until Election Day, both Kamala Harris and Donald Trump are setting their sights on the key battleground state of Michigan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How Republicans mainstreamed the baseless idea of noncitizen voting in 2024
item.description: Donald Trump and his allies have zeroed in on the baseless claim that Democrats are encouraging newly arrived migrants to vote for them in the 2024 election. There is no evidence of a plot like this.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block and Miles Parks, MPR News

item.title: Haitian migration roils town in key battleground state with signs of pro-Trump support on the rise
item.description: The town of Charleroi has come under scrutiny after President Trump pointed to an influx of Haitian immigration into the small town in the battleground state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris and Trump seek Arab American votes in Michigan in effort to shore up battleground states
item.description: Kamala Harris and Donald Trump are both seeking support from Arab American voters as they campaign in Michigan, trying to lock down support in a battleground state that could decide the presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: At Wisconsin stops, Harris campaign features less talk of joy and more attacks on Trump
item.description: In La Crosse on Thursday, Harris told a rally crowd that Trump “will stop at nothing to claim unchecked power for himself.” It’s a shift from the “joy” that swirled around her elevation to the top of the Democratic ticket this summer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: 3 states renew efforts to limit abortion pill access
item.description: The lawsuit was filed in a Texas federal court Friday, roughly four months after the Supreme Court decided against undoing changes to the FDA’s regulations on the abortion pill that expanded access — such as allowing it to be mailed without an in-person doctor’s visit.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, Fox 5

item.title: Biden administration forgives student loans for 60,000 public service workers
item.description: The Biden administration announced Thursday that it is forgiving the student loans for more than 60,000 public service workers. The move gives $4.5 billion in debt relief to teachers, firefighters and other eligible workers who have been paying on their loans for more than 10 years.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance says 'no,' he does not think Trump lost in 2020
item.description: Vance has faced repeated questions on the campaign trail about whether Trump lost in 2020. On Wednesday, he gave his most direct answer to date, answering "no" in response to a reporter's question.
item.dc:creator: Jason Breslow, MPR News

item.title: DENIED: How one Tampa Bay couple recovers after Hurricane Helene
item.description: Floridians across the coast and beyond are feeling overwhelmed with the FEMA application process and denial while struggling on the road to recovery, calling this a “nightmare” situation.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Harris interview on Fox gets testy — but also gives her a do-over
item.description: The interview was the vice president's first formal sit-down with Fox. She faced questions on immigration, and was given a chance to answer again what she would do differently from President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith, MPR News

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: The Nebraska Supreme Court rejected the contention that the state’s pardon board has sole authority to restore such rights.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Lyft launches voter transportation initiative
item.description: “Lyft believes transportation access should never be a barrier for any citizen seeking to vote," Lyft Chief Policy Officer Jerry Golden said in the release.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Harris campaign hosts separated families ahead of Trump's Univision town hall
item.description: The Harris campaign has mostly centered its immigration and border security messaging on enforcement, drawing some criticism from advocates.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Canceling subscriptions has to be as easy as signing up, FTC says in a new rule
item.description: The "click-to-cancel" rule, now finalized by the Federal Trade Commission, aims to make it easier to cancel subscriptions and memberships. Companies argue the agency overstepped its authority.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh, MPR News

item.title: Lisa Cole, Nevada Assembly District 4 candidate, discusses economic development, affordable housing, and education
item.description: Lisa Cole, a candidate for Nevada Assembly District 4, appeared on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide to discuss her platform.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Shannon Bilbray-Axelrod discusses growth, traffic, and affordable housing in county commissioner race
item.description: Shannon Bilbray-Axelrod, a candidate for county commissioner and current Nevada Assembly member, discussed her key priorities for her district on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate rankings: 5 seats most likely to flip
item.description: The battle for the Senate majority has hit its final month as Republicans race to end their four-year stint in the minority. The GOP needs to win only one of two competitive races in red states — Montana and Ohio — to do just that, barring a surprise loss elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Trump hears at a Latino campaign event from someone who lived in the US illegally
item.description: Minutes after Donald Trump delivered his standard warnings about drug dealers and criminals illegally crossing the border during a Saturday campaign event, the former president heard from someone who was once in the country illegally but now plans to vote for him.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, Fox 5
